---
title: "GPU Cost Attribution"
subtitle: "Factory overhead-absorption, pointed at a shared GPU cluster"
author: "Theerayut (TJ) Jaiyen"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, theme.scss]
    embed-resources: true
    slide-number: true
    footer: "TJ Jaiyen · linkedin.com/in/jaiyentheerayut"
    transition: fade
    width: 1280
    height: 720
jupyter: python3
---

## The idea in one line {.center}

> Idle GPU capacity is **factory overhead**. You absorb it across tenants by an allocation key
> (token volume) — exactly like spreading shared equipment cost across job orders by machine-hours.

The cost-accounting discipline, pointed at **AI unit economics**.

::: notes
This is the FinOps extension of the finance-variance work — same method, different ledger.
:::

## The pipeline

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  U[raw_api_usage] --> A[int_tenant_allocation]
  B[raw_gpu_billing] --> C[int_daily_cluster_cost]
  K[raw_contracts] --> A
  A --> M[fct_daily_tenant_costs]
  C --> M
  M --> Z[per-tenant margin]
```

`fleet_type` keeps **training compute (R&D) out of COGS** — only the inference fleet is absorbed.

## The absorption method

- **Idle pool** = provisioned − used GPU hours → the overhead to absorb
- **Allocation key** = `tenant_tokens / daily_total_tokens` → each tenant's share
- **Revenue** = tokens × contract rates; **margin** = revenue − absorbed cost

. . .

| Cost accounting | → | FinOps |
|---|---|---|
| overhead absorption | → | idle-capacity allocation |
| standard-vs-actual variance | → | spend anomaly |
| subledger-to-GL reconciliation | → | `relationships` test |

## Which tenant is unprofitable — and why {.smaller}

```{python}
#| label: margin-chart
#| echo: false
#| fig-align: center
import pandas as pd, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from pathlib import Path

mart = Path("data/gpu_tenant_costs.csv")   # exported from `dbt build` (fct_daily_tenant_costs)
if mart.exists():
    m = pd.read_csv(mart)
    g = m.groupby("customer_id").agg(rev=("revenue","sum"), cost=("total_cost","sum")).reset_index()
    source = "dbt mart · fct_daily_tenant_costs (dbt build → 33 tests pass)"
else:  # fallback so the deck always renders without a dbt build
    b = "../dbt_gpu_cost_attribution/seeds"
    bill = pd.read_csv(f"{b}/raw_gpu_billing.csv"); use = pd.read_csv(f"{b}/raw_api_usage.csv"); con = pd.read_csv(f"{b}/raw_contracts.csv")
    inf = bill[bill.fleet_type=="inference"].copy(); inf["c"]=inf.hours_provisioned*inf.hourly_rate
    dc = inf.groupby("usage_date")["c"].sum().rename("cluster")
    use["tok"]=use.input_tokens+use.output_tokens
    dt = use.groupby("usage_date")["tok"].sum().rename("day"); u = use.merge(dt,on="usage_date").merge(dc,on="usage_date")
    u["cost"]=(u.tok/u.day)*u.cluster; u = u.merge(con,on="customer_id")
    u["rev"]=u.input_tokens*u.input_token_rate+u.output_tokens*u.output_token_rate
    g = u.groupby("customer_id").agg(rev=("rev","sum"), cost=("cost","sum")).reset_index()
    source = "recomputed from seeds (no dbt build present)"

g["margin_pct"] = (g.rev - g.cost) / g.rev * 100
g = g.sort_values("margin_pct")
colors = ["#c0392b" if v < 40 else ("#1e8449" if v > 65 else "#b7950b") for v in g.margin_pct]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4.3))
ax.bar(g.customer_id, g.margin_pct, color=colors, width=0.55)
ax.axhline(0, color="#888", lw=0.8); ax.axhline(40, color="#bbb", ls="--", lw=0.7)
ax.set_ylabel("Gross margin (%)"); ax.set_title("Per-tenant gross margin — absorbed GPU cost vs contract revenue")
for s in ["top","right"]: ax.spines[s].set_visible(False)
for x, v in zip(g.customer_id, g.margin_pct):
    ax.text(x, v + (2 if v>=0 else -4), f"{v:+.0f}%", ha="center", fontsize=10)
ax.text(0.99, -0.18, source, transform=ax.transAxes, ha="right", fontsize=7, color="#9aa0a6")
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

`cust_c`'s contract rates don't cover its absorbed compute — **negative margin** (warning zone);
`cust_b` is best-in-class. These are the **dbt mart's own figures**, every one verified by `dbt build`.

## Governed by tests, not trust

Three tiers, all run by `dbt build` — **nothing ships if one fails**:

- **Data tests** — not_null / unique key, `relationships` (every tenant exists in contracts),
  `accepted_values` on `fleet_type` and `margin_zone`
- **Unit test** — pins the allocation + margin *logic* against mocked inputs in CI
- **Singular tests** — allocation ratios **sum to 1** per day (nothing lost or double-counted);
  **no negative costs**

## Why it matters {.center}

The "**which customer is unprofitable, and why**" question — answered by **tested, auditable models**,
not a spreadsheet.

Same discipline as the GL variance work: facts in SQL, governed by tests; AI only where it earns trust.

— Theerayut (TJ) Jaiyen · [linkedin.com/in/jaiyentheerayut](https://linkedin.com/in/jaiyentheerayut)

::: notes
Synthetic data only — GPU rates are illustrative point-in-time 2026.
:::
